what_it_does: Defines a static HTML/CSS phone mockup for a decision-card compare pattern, showing a merge-strategy choice between Absorb and Fork with a highlighted recommended option, relation joint, explanation block, and skip link.
observations: The page imports Plus Jakarta Sans, IBM Plex Mono, shared piece and phone styles, and /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
The visible mockup is a single phone screen titled DECISION / Merge Strategy with the lead question "How should Wildcard's work enter the union?"
Two .dc-take option cards are shown: Absorb marked Recommended and Fork marked Option B.
The recommended option uses gold border, goldwash background, and a visible Choose Absorb action bar.
A vertical .dc-joint connector sits between the two option cards using socket, dashed line, and plug shapes.
A "Why this matters" block explains the prototype-to-union framing.
The CSS includes hover, recommended, selected, choose-bar, joint, why, done, skip, and hatch styling.
The .dc-done completion style is defined but no matching .dc-done markup appears in the body.
standouts: The compare relation is expressed visually through a connector between the two option cards.
Recommendation and selection states share a gold/goldwash treatment and reveal an explicit choose action.
The file combines decision content, phone-frame presentation, and component-specific styling in one local HTML artifact.
conventions: Uses shared design tokens through CSS variables such as --dna-space-24, --dna-type-label, --gold, and --line.
Uses dc- prefixed class names for the component-specific styles.
Uses IBM Plex Mono for tags and metadata-like labels.
Keeps the piece self-contained with inline CSS while relying on shared surface and phone styles.
Uses div-based interactive-looking cards rather than semantic form controls.
concerns: The option cards have cursor and hover states but are non-semantic divs with no local click handling or keyboard affordance.
The "Decide later" element is an anchor without an href.
The .dc-done and .selected styles are present without matching local markup or behavior.
The recommended choice appears active visually, but the file itself does not define selection state changes.
notes: This file is a self-contained compare-decision phone mockup centered on choosing how prototype work enters a union.
questions: Is the selected state meant to be driven by /surface/runtime/organisms.js or kept as a static visual state?
Should "Decide later" navigate somewhere, trigger a state change, or remain a visual placeholder?
Is .dc-done intended as an alternate completion state for this same piece?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated title and visible role describe a revised compare archetype, and the file implements a two-option decision comparison with a relation joint.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to test a compact mobile decision pattern where two strategic options are compared, one option is recommended, and the relationship between choices is made visible rather than only textual.
novelty: The connector between option cards gives the comparison a physical joint metaphor inside an otherwise card-based decision UI.
contribution: Contributes a mobile compare-decision substrate with recommended and selected visual states, explicit choice bars, a relation connector between alternatives, explanatory rationale copy, and skip affordance that a fused system could preserve as decision-comparison vocabulary.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS phone mockup of a decision-card compare archetype with Absorb versus Fork options, recommended styling, a visual joint connector, rationale block, and skip link.
intention_for_embedding: Created to explore how a mobile decision UI can present two merge-strategy paths with a recommendation, visible relation, and concise rationale.
